ListItem.Para: Valve is deactivated closed.
Opposite trim air pressure regulator valve operates normally.
EDTO is not conducted.

Note.Para: Cabin zone trim valves and their associated trim air PRSOVs are shown below.

Para.Emphasis: Associated Trim Air Pressure Regulator Valve

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Deactivate the inoperative cabin zone trim valve closed (AMM DDG 21-61-04).

ListItem.Para: For cabin zones B or D:
Set left air conditioning pack OFF.
Set the GND TEST selector to ENABLE no less than 30-seconds prior to opening the Pack Control Unit circuit breakers.
Open the following circuit breakers and lock with a DO NOT CLOSE tag.
PACK CTRL UNIT- CHAN L1
PACK CTRL UNIT- CHAN L2
Gain access to the zone trim valve.
Disconnect, cap and stow the electrical connector for the applicable zone trim valve.
Turn the open/close knob until the valve is in the closed position.
Close the following circuit breakers:
PACK CTRL UNIT- CHAN L1
PACK CTRL UNIT- CHAN L2
Return the GND TEST selector to NORM.
Set pack as desired.
For cabin zones A or C:
Set right air conditioning pack OFF.
Set the GND TEST selector to ENABLE no less than 30-seconds prior to opening the Pack Control Unit circuit breakers.
Open the following circuit breakers and lock with a DO NOT CLOSE tag.
PACK CTRL UNIT- CHAN R1
PACK CTRL UNIT- CHAN R2
Gain access to the zone trim valve.
Disconnect, cap and stow the electrical connector for the applicable zone trim valve.
Turn the open/close knob until the valve is in the closed position.
Close the following circuit breakers:
PACK CTRL UNIT- CHAN R1
PACK CTRL UNIT- CHAN R2
Return the GND TEST selector to NORM.
Set pack as desired.
Trim air is not available in the cabin zone associated with the deactivated trim air valve.
If cabin or flight deck temperatures become too cool, climbing to a higher altitude will increase the heating capability of the air conditioning system.
